subject: Add rtl8821au, rtl88x2bu, rtl8821cu WiFi drivers.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: No impact on our build — this commit is the v5.13.12 instance of the vendored-wifi lineage, not carried forward to our 6.18 kernel build.

forward_port.conflicts: vendored ~2021 Realtek HAL trees do not build against 6.18; fork abandoned this tree revision one commit later (3740d5b88) in favor of actively-maintained morrownr forks and mainline coverage.

superseded_by: 33ff5146a7248ef86e15bb3b78f1f7516f86ee4f (v5.15 expanded version: adds rtl8188eu/rtl8188fu in addition to this commit's three drivers; supersedes this v5.13 lineage instance)

license_provenance.author: Realtek Semiconductor Corporation
origin_projects: RTL8821AU: Realtek vendor tree, combined RTL8812A/RTL8821A HAL
RTL88X2BU: Realtek vendor tree, RTL8812B/RTL8822B family

notes:
v5.13.12 instance of the vendored-wifi lineage. Three-driver precursor to the v5.15 expanded version (33ff5146a), which adds rtl8188eu and rtl8188fu. Not carried as a patch in our build; superseded by v5.15 commit within fork history and ultimately by mainline kernel drivers (rtw88) and out-of-tree BR2 packages (rtl8812au, rtl8821au-morrownr). Comprehensive disposition and coverage analysis in record 33ff5146a7248ef86e15bb3b78f1f7516f86ee4f.json.

sonnet-verified: correct. Branch containment confirmed (remotes/origin/MiSTer-v5.13.12 only, via `git branch --contains`); date (2021-08-24) and subject match `git show -s`; added/removed (1813056/0, 1685 files) match `git show --stat` exactly. This record's affected_hardware/superseded_by correctly distinguish RTL8812A (BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8812AU) from RTL8821A (BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821AU_MORROWNR) as two separate chip families within the combined rtl8821au tree — unlike its sibling record c70a3fc27 (same driver payload, v5.14 branch), which had mislabeled one of the two as 'RTL8811A' and omitted the RTL8812AU package; that sibling has been corrected to match this record's (already-accurate) framing. No changes needed here. The empty upstream.*/device_ids fields and explicit deferral to 33ff5146a's fuller analysis are an intentional, reasonable 'lineage brief' given three near-identical commits (3d587b6a → c70a3fc27 → 33ff5146a) cover the same driver trees with increasing scope; duplicating the full grounding in each would be redundant, not a grounding gap.
